records.ety: [L. intercalatus, p. p. of intercalare to intercalate to intercalate; inter between + calare to call, proclaim. See Calendar.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Intercalated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Intercalating (?).]

defs: To insert, as a day or other portion of time, in a calendar.
To insert among others, as a verse in a stanza; specif. (Geol.), to introduce as a bed or stratum, between the layers of a regular series of rocks.
quotes: Beds of fresh-water shells . . . are intercalated and interstratified with the shale.
